SSR and Client Boundary Rules 118 119 - The provider renders a `
` wrapper during SSR with the initial direction value. 120 - All context values are SSR-safe (no browser dependencies in the context itself). 121 - Platform-specific operations go through `PlatformEffects`, not through ArsProvider. 122 123 ## 15. Performance Constraints 124 125 - Provided context should only update when incoming signal values actually change. 126 - The `direction` memo only recomputes when locale or direction prop changes. 127 - Do not recreate context values unnecessarily on unrelated rerenders. 128 129 ## 16. Implementation Dependencies 130 131 | Dependency | Required? | Dependency type | Why it must exist first | Notes | 132 | ------------- | ----------- | ------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | 133 | `dismissable` | recommended | downstream consumer | Dismissable-like utilities often consume environment context for scoped DOM access. | Dependency is about implementation order in the wider utility layer, not runtime ownership. | 134 | `focus-scope` | recommended | downstream consumer | Focus utilities may rely on scoped environment data in composite trees. | Same guidance applies to future overlay utilities. | 135 136 ## 17. Recommended Implementation Sequence 137 138 1. Resolve all props to signals (defaulting absent signals to stored defaults). 139 2. Derive `direction` memo from locale when direction prop is `None`. 140 3. Build and publish provider-owned `ArsNonceCssCtx`. 141 4. Build and publish `ArsContext` via `provide_context`. 142 5. Render `
{children()}
`. 143 6. Verify descendant consumption and provider cleanup. 144 145 ## 18. Anti-Patterns 146 147 - Do not fail hard when optional props are absent — use documented defaults. 148 - Do not add heavy DOM structure beyond the `
` wrapper. 149 150 ## 19. Consumer Expectations and Guarantees 151 152 - Consumers may assume `ArsContext` is always present when rendered inside an `ArsProvider`. 153 - Consumers may assume `use_locale()` returns a valid locale signal even without a provider (fallback to `en-US`). 154 - Consumers may assume the `dir` attribute on the wrapper reflects the current direction. 155 156 ## 20. Platform Support Matrix 157 158 | Capability / behavior | Browser client | SSR | Notes | 159 | ----------------------- | -------------- | ------------ | ------------------------------------------------- | 160 | context publication | full support | full support | All context values are platform-agnostic. | 161 | `dir` wrapper rendering | full support | full support | Renders `
` in both client and SSR paths. | 162 163 ## 21. Debug Diagnostics and Production Policy 164 165 | Condition | Debug build behavior | Production behavior | Notes | 166 | --------------------------- | -------------------- | ------------------- | ---------------------------------------- | 167 | no ArsProvider in ancestors | debug warning | degrade gracefully | `use_locale()` returns fallback `en-US`. | 168 169 ## 22. Shared Adapter Helper Notes 170 171 | Helper concept | Required? | Responsibility | Reused by | Notes | 172 | --------------------------- | --------- | ------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------- | 173 | `use_locale()` hook | required | Read locale from ArsContext with en-US fallback | all locale-dependent components | Defined in `08-adapter-leptos.md` §13.1 | 174 | `use_direction()` hook | required | Read direction from ArsContext with Ltr fallback | all direction-dependent components | Parallel to `use_locale()` | 175 | `use_style_strategy()` hook | required | Read style strategy from ArsContext | all components using `attr_map_to_leptos()` | Defined in `08-adapter-leptos.md` §3.4 | 176 177 ## 23. Framework-Specific Behavior 178 179 Leptos is web-only. All `ArsContext` values are platform-agnostic. Platform-specific operations (focus, scroll-lock, positioning) go through `PlatformEffects`, not ArsProvider. 180 181 ## 24. Canonical Implementation Sketch 182 183 ```rust 184 #[component] 185 pub fn ArsProvider( 186 #[prop(optional, into)] locale: Option>, 187 #[prop(optional, into)] direction: Option>, 188 #[prop(optional, into)] color_mode: Option>, 189 #[prop(optional, into)] disabled: Option>, 190 #[prop(optional, into)] read_only: Option>, 191 #[prop(optional)] id_prefix: Option, 192 #[prop(optional)] portal_container_id: Option, 193 #[prop(optional)] root_node_id: Option, 194 #[prop(optional)] platform: Option>, 195 #[prop(optional)] intl_backend: Option>, 196 #[prop(optional)] i18n_registries: Option>, 197 #[prop(optional)] style_strategy: Option, 198 children: Children, 199 ) -> impl IntoView { 200 let locale = locale.unwrap_or_else(|| { 201 Signal::stored(Locale::parse("en-US").expect("en-US is always valid")) 202 }); 203 let direction = Memo::new(move |_| { 204 direction 205 .map(|d| d.get()) 206 .unwrap_or_else(|| locale.get().direction()) 207 }); 208 let color_mode = color_mode.unwrap_or_else(|| Signal::stored(ColorMode::System)); 209 let disabled = disabled.unwrap_or_else(|| Signal::stored(false)); 210 let read_only = read_only.unwrap_or_else(|| Signal::stored(false)); 211 let platform = platform.unwrap_or_else(|| Rc::new(WebPlatformEffects)); 212 let intl_backend = intl_backend.unwrap_or_else(|| Arc::new(StubIntlBackend)); 213 let i18n_registries = i18n_registries.unwrap_or_else(|| Rc::new(I18nRegistries::new())); 214 let style_strategy = style_strategy.unwrap_or(StyleStrategy::Inline); 215 216 provide_context(ArsNonceCssCtx { 217 rules: RwSignal::new(Vec::::new()), 218 }); 219 220 provide_context(ArsContext { 221 locale, 222 direction, 223 color_mode, 224 disabled, 225 read_only, 226 id_prefix: Signal::stored(id_prefix), 227 portal_container_id: Signal::stored(portal_container_id), 228 root_node_id: Signal::stored(root_node_id), 229 platform, 230 intl_backend, 231 i18n_registries, 232 style_strategy, 233 }); 234 235 let dir_attr = move || direction.get().as_html_attr(); 236 237 view! { 238
239 {children()} 240
241 } 242 } 243 ``` 244 245 ## 25. Adapter Invariants 246 247 - Context publication and consumption rules must remain explicit so descendants know when environment data is available. 248 - The `dir` attribute on the wrapper MUST reactively update when direction changes. 249 - `use_locale()` MUST always return a valid signal, even without a provider. 250 251 ## 26. Accessibility and SSR Notes 252 253 The `
` wrapper propagates reading direction for correct BiDi layout. No ARIA semantics. SSR renders the wrapper with initial direction value. 254 255 ## 27. Parity Summary and Intentional Deviations 256 257 Parity summary: full core context parity. 258 259 Intentional deviations: none. 260 261 ## 28. Test Scenarios 262 263 - context publication with all props 264 - nonce collector publication for `ArsNonceStyle` 265 - default fallback values when props are absent 266 - `use_locale()` fallback without provider 267 - `dir` attribute reactivity on locale change 268 - SSR rendering 269 - `use_style_strategy()` returns `StyleStrategy::Inline` by default 270 - `use_style_strategy()` returns configured strategy when explicitly set 271 272 ## 29. Test Oracle Notes 273 274 | Behavior | Preferred oracle type | Notes | 275 | ---------------------- | --------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------- | 276 | provider publication | context registration | Assert descendants observe the published ArsContext. | 277 | locale fallback | context registration | Assert `use_locale()` returns en-US without a provider. | 278 | dir attribute | DOM assertion | Assert `dir` attribute matches current direction. | 279 | style strategy default | context registration | Assert `use_style_strategy()` returns `Inline` without explicit prop. | 280 | nonce collector | context registration | Assert `append_nonce_css()` collects rules from provider context. | 281 282 ## 30. Implementation Checklist 283 284 - [ ] Provider context publishes all documented `ArsContext` fields. 285 - [ ] Provider publishes an `ArsNonceCssCtx` collector for `ArsNonceStyle`. 286 - [ ] `
` wrapper renders and reactively updates. 287 - [ ] Default fallback values match documented defaults (en-US, Ltr, System, false, false, Inline). 288 - [ ] `use_locale()` works with and without provider. 289 - [ ] `use_style_strategy()` returns `StyleStrategy::Inline` by default. 290 - [ ] SSR renders the wrapper with correct `dir`. 291 - [ ] Context-registration test oracles are covered. 292

1	---
2	adapter: dioxus
3	component: ars-provider
4	category: utility
5	source: components/utility/ars-provider.md
6	source_foundation: foundation/09-adapter-dioxus.md
7	---
8	
9	# ArsProvider — Dioxus Adapter
10	
11	## 1. Purpose and Adapter Scope
12	
13	This spec maps the core [`ArsProvider`](../../components/utility/ars-provider.md) context contract to Dioxus 0.7.x. `ArsProvider` is the single root provider — it subsumes the formerly separate `LocaleProvider`, `ArsStyleProvider`, and `PlatformProvider`.
14	
15	## 2. Public Adapter API
16	
17	```rust,no_check
18	#[derive(Props, Clone)]
19	pub struct ArsProviderProps {
20	    #[props(optional, into)]
21	    pub locale: Option<Signal<Locale>>,
22	    #[props(optional, into)]
23	    pub direction: Option<Signal<Direction>>,
24	    #[props(optional, into)]
25	    pub color_mode: Option<Signal<ColorMode>>,
26	    #[props(optional, into)]
27	    pub disabled: Option<Signal<bool>>,
28	    #[props(optional, into)]
29	    pub read_only: Option<Signal<bool>>,
30	    #[props(optional)]
31	    pub id_prefix: Option<String>,
32	    #[props(optional)]
33	    pub portal_container_id: Option<String>,
34	    #[props(optional)]
35	    pub root_node_id: Option<String>,
36	    #[props(optional)]
37	    pub platform: Option<Arc<dyn PlatformEffects>>,
38	    #[props(optional)]
39	    pub intl_backend: Option<Arc<dyn IntlBackend>>,
40	    #[props(optional)]
41	    pub i18n_registries: Option<Arc<I18nRegistries>>,
42	    #[props(optional)]
43	    pub style_strategy: Option<StyleStrategy>,
44	    #[props(optional)]
45	    pub dioxus_platform: Option<Arc<dyn DioxusPlatform>>,
46	    pub children: Element,
47	}
48	
49	impl PartialEq for ArsProviderProps {
50	    fn eq(&self, other: &Self) -> bool;
51	}
52	
53	#[component]
54	pub fn ArsProvider(props: ArsProviderProps) -> Element
55	```
56	
57	## 3. Mapping to Core Component Contract
58	
59	- Props parity: full parity with the core context props. Reactive props (`locale`, `direction`, `color_mode`, `disabled`, `read_only`) accept `Signal` for live updates. Non-reactive props (`id_prefix`, `portal_container_id`, `root_node_id`) are plain values set at mount time.
60	- `style_strategy` maps directly to the core `StyleStrategy` prop (defaults to `StyleStrategy::Inline`).
61	- `dioxus_platform` is an adapter-only extra prop (no core equivalent) — it provides Dioxus-specific platform services (file pickers, clipboard, drag data).
62	- `ArsProviderProps` must implement `Clone + PartialEq`, but `PartialEq` may be implemented manually so trait-object fields can use adapter-defined semantic equality.
63	- Context parity: publishes full `ArsContext` and the adapter nonce CSS collector to descendants via `use_context_provider`.
64	
65	## 4. Part Mapping
66	
67	| Core part / structure | Required?     | Adapter rendering target | Ownership      | Attr source | Notes                                 |
68	| --------------------- | ------------- | ------------------------ | -------------- | ----------- | ------------------------------------- |
69	| ars provider boundary | provider-only | `<div dir="{dir}">`      | adapter-owned  | direction   | Wrapper propagates `dir` attribute.   |
70	| children subtree      | required      | consumer children        | consumer-owned | none        | Descendants consume provided context. |
71	
72	## 5. Attr Merge and Ownership Rules
73	
74	| Target node     | Core attrs | Adapter-owned attrs            | Consumer attrs | Merge order    | Ownership notes                                |
75	| --------------- | ---------- | ------------------------------ | -------------- | -------------- | ---------------------------------------------- |
76	| `<div>` wrapper | none       | `dir` (derived from direction) | none           | not applicable | Minimal wrapper for BiDi direction propagation |
77	
78	## 6. Composition / Context Contract
79	
80	The adapter publishes `ArsContext` with `use_context_provider`. It also publishes an `ArsNonceCssCtx` collector owned by the provider instance and automatically renders `ArsNonceStyle` when `style_strategy` is `StyleStrategy::Nonce(nonce)`. Consumers use `try_use_context::<ArsContext>()`. The convenience hooks `use_locale()` and `use_direction()` read from this context with fallback defaults. During Dioxus SSR, all context values are platform-agnostic and require no browser globals.
81	
82	## 7. Prop Sync and Event Mapping
83	
84	ArsProvider is context-driven rather than event-driven.
85	
86	| Adapter prop                                         | Mode       | Sync trigger            | Update path                       | Visible effect                               | Notes                                                                     |
87	| ---------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | ----------------------- | --------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
88	| `locale`                                             | controlled | signal change           | update ArsContext.locale          | descendants see updated locale               | direction auto-inferred when direction=None                               |
89	| `direction`                                          | controlled | signal change           | update ArsContext.direction       | `dir` attribute updates, layout reflows      | overrides locale-inferred direction                                       |
90	| `color_mode`                                         | controlled | signal change           | update ArsContext.color_mode      | theme-aware descendants re-render            |                                                                           |
91	| `disabled` / `read_only`                             | controlled | signal change           | update ArsContext fields          | descendant components enable/disable         |                                                                           |
92	| `id_prefix` / `portal_container_id` / `root_node_id` | controlled | prop change after mount | update provided context           | descendants resolve new ID prefix/targets    | non-reactive; set once at mount                                           |
93	| `style_strategy`                                     | controlled | prop set at mount       | update ArsContext.style_strategy  | descendant components use strategy for CSS   | defaults to `StyleStrategy::Inline`; `Nonce` auto-renders the nonce style |
94	| `dioxus_platform`                                    | controlled | prop set at mount       | update ArsContext.dioxus_platform | adapter-specific platform services available | defaults via feature flag resolution                                      |
95	| nonce CSS collector                                  | internal   | provider mount          | provide `ArsNonceCssCtx`          | provider-owned style renders rules           | provider-owned                                                            |
96	
97	## 8. Registration and Cleanup Contract
98	
99	- No DOM registration lifecycle beyond the `<div>` wrapper.
100	- Cleanup is the lifetime of the provided context value only.
101	- Descendants must stop reading provider data after the provider unmounts.
102	
103	| Registered entity   | Registration trigger | Identity key      | Cleanup trigger  | Cleanup action     | Notes                                             |
104	| ------------------- | -------------------- | ----------------- | ---------------- | ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------- |
105	| provided ArsContext | provider mount       | provider instance | provider cleanup | drop context value | no DOM listener ownership in the provider itself  |
106	| nonce CSS collector | provider mount       | provider instance | provider cleanup | drop context value | rendered automatically for `StyleStrategy::Nonce` |
107	
108	## 9. Ref and Node Contract
109	
110	| Target part / node | Ref required? | Ref owner      | Node availability | Composition rule | Notes                                             |
111	| ------------------ | ------------- | -------------- | ----------------- | ---------------- | ------------------------------------------------- |
112	| `<div>` wrapper    | no            | not applicable | server-safe       | not applicable   | Renders a minimal `dir` wrapper on all platforms. |
113	
114	## 10. State Machine Boundary Rules
115	
116	- machine-owned state: provided context values exposed through `ArsContext`.
117	- adapter-local derived bookkeeping: `Memo<Direction>` derived from locale signal when direction prop is `None`.
118	- forbidden local mirrors: do not keep separate unsynchronized copies outside the published context.
119	- allowed snapshot-read contexts: provider render/effects only.
120	
121	## 11. Callback Payload Contract
122	
123	| Callback           | Payload source | Payload shape | Timing         | Cancelable? | Notes                                                      |
124	| ------------------ | -------------- | ------------- | -------------- | ----------- | ---------------------------------------------------------- |
125	| no public callback | none           | none          | not applicable | no          | Descendants consume context rather than callback payloads. |
126	
127	## 12. Failure and Degradation Rules
128	
129	| Condition                                                   | Policy             | Notes                                                                                  |
130	| ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
131	| locale signal absent (None prop)                            | use default        | Default to `en-US`, direction inferred as `Ltr`.                                       |
132	| descendant expects required environment data that is absent | warn and ignore    | The provider remains valid; required-descendant behavior belongs to the consumer spec. |
133	| platform unavailable during SSR or server-only rendering    | degrade gracefully | All context values are platform-agnostic; no platform lookup needed.                   |
134	| `style_strategy` absent                                     | use default        | Default to `StyleStrategy::Inline`.                                                    |
135	| `dioxus_platform` absent                                    | use default        | Resolved via feature flags: Web > Desktop > NullPlatform.                              |
136	
137	## 13. Identity and Key Policy
138	
139	| Registered or repeated structure | Identity source  | Duplicates allowed? | DOM order must match registration order? | SSR/hydration stability                                        | Notes                                       |
140	| -------------------------------- | ---------------- | ------------------- | ---------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------- |
141	| provided ArsContext              | instance-derived | not applicable      | not applicable                           | provider instance identity must remain stable across hydration | Provider lifetime is the identity boundary. |
142	
143	## 14. SSR and Client Boundary Rules
144	
145	- The provider renders a `<div dir>` wrapper during SSR with the initial direction value.
146	- All context values are platform-agnostic (no browser dependencies in the context itself).
147	- Platform-specific operations go through `PlatformEffects` or `DioxusPlatform`, not through ArsProvider.
148	- On server rendering, no platform lookup is required — ArsProvider publishes plain configuration values.
149	
150	## 15. Performance Constraints
151	
152	- Provided context should only update when incoming signal values actually change.
153	- The `direction` memo only recomputes when locale or direction prop changes.
154	- Chain `dir` attribute off the `direction` memo to avoid redundant re-reads of `locale`.
155	
156	## 16. Implementation Dependencies
157	
158	| Dependency    | Required?   | Dependency type     | Why it must exist first                                                             | Notes                                                                                       |
159	| ------------- | ----------- | ------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
160	| `dismissable` | recommended | downstream consumer | Dismissable-like utilities often consume environment context for scoped DOM access. | Dependency is about implementation order in the wider utility layer, not runtime ownership. |
161	| `focus-scope` | recommended | downstream consumer | Focus utilities may rely on scoped environment data in composite trees.             | Same guidance applies to future overlay utilities.                                          |
162	
163	## 17. Recommended Implementation Sequence
164	
165	1. Resolve all props to signals (defaulting absent signals to stored defaults).
166	2. Derive `direction` memo from locale when direction prop is `None`.
167	3. Resolve `style_strategy` (default `StyleStrategy::Inline`).
168	4. Resolve `dioxus_platform` via feature flag fallback (Web > Desktop > NullPlatform).
169	5. Build and publish provider-owned `ArsNonceCssCtx`.
170	6. Build and publish `ArsContext` via `use_context_provider`.
171	7. Render `div { dir: "{dir}", {children} }`.
172	8. Verify descendant consumption and provider cleanup.
173	
174	## 18. Anti-Patterns
175	
176	- Do not fail hard when optional props are absent — use documented defaults.
177	- Do not add heavy DOM structure beyond the `<div dir>` wrapper.
178	- Do not attempt platform lookup (e.g., `use_platform()`) inside ArsProvider — it provides configuration, not platform capabilities.
179	
180	## 19. Consumer Expectations and Guarantees
181	
182	- Consumers may assume `ArsContext` is always present when rendered inside an `ArsProvider`.
183	- Consumers may assume `use_locale()` returns a valid locale signal even without a provider (fallback to `en-US`).
184	- Consumers may assume the `dir` attribute on the wrapper reflects the current direction.
185	
186	## 20. Platform Support Matrix
187	
188	| Capability / behavior   | Web          | Desktop      | Mobile       | SSR          | Notes                                      |
189	| ----------------------- | ------------ | ------------ | ------------ | ------------ | ------------------------------------------ |
190	| context publication     | full support | full support | full support | full support | All context values are platform-agnostic.  |
191	| `dir` wrapper rendering | full support | full support | full support | full support | Renders `<div dir>` on all Dioxus targets. |
192	
193	## 21. Debug Diagnostics and Production Policy
194	
195	| Condition                   | Debug build behavior | Production behavior | Notes                                    |
196	| --------------------------- | -------------------- | ------------------- | ---------------------------------------- |
197	| no ArsProvider in ancestors | debug warning        | degrade gracefully  | `use_locale()` returns fallback `en-US`. |
198	
199	## 22. Shared Adapter Helper Notes
200	
201	| Helper concept      | Required? | Responsibility                                  | Reused by                       | Notes                                   |
202	| ------------------- | --------- | ----------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------- | --------------------------------------- |
203	| `use_locale()` hook | required  | Read locale from ArsContext with en-US fallback | all locale-dependent components | Defined in `09-adapter-dioxus.md` §16.1 |
204	
205	## 23. Framework-Specific Behavior
206	
207	Dioxus targets web, desktop, and mobile. `ArsContext` bundles platform-agnostic configuration, the `PlatformEffects` trait object, and the Dioxus-specific `DioxusPlatform` trait object. The `dioxus_platform` prop is an adapter-only extra with no core equivalent — it provides Dioxus-specific platform services (file pickers, clipboard, drag data) not covered by `PlatformEffects`. The Dioxus adapter resolves the platform implementation via feature flags: `WebPlatform` (web), `DesktopPlatform` (desktop), `NullPlatform` (SSR/tests/mobile fallback). The `style_strategy` field controls CSS injection for all descendant ars components.
208	
209	## 24. Canonical Implementation Sketch
210	
211	```rust
212	#[derive(Props, Clone)]
213	pub struct ArsProviderSketchProps {
214	    #[props(optional, into)]
215	    pub locale: Option<Signal<Locale>>,
216	    #[props(optional, into)]
217	    pub direction: Option<Signal<Direction>>,
218	    #[props(optional, into)]
219	    pub color_mode: Option<Signal<ColorMode>>,
220	    #[props(optional, into)]
221	    pub disabled: Option<Signal<bool>>,
222	    #[props(optional, into)]
223	    pub read_only: Option<Signal<bool>>,
224	    #[props(optional)]
225	    pub id_prefix: Option<String>,
226	    #[props(optional)]
227	    pub portal_container_id: Option<String>,
228	    #[props(optional)]
229	    pub root_node_id: Option<String>,
230	    #[props(optional)]
231	    pub platform: Option<Arc<dyn PlatformEffects>>,
232	    #[props(optional)]
233	    pub intl_backend: Option<Arc<dyn IntlBackend>>,
234	    #[props(optional)]
235	    pub i18n_registries: Option<Arc<I18nRegistries>>,
236	    #[props(optional)]
237	    pub style_strategy: Option<StyleStrategy>,
238	    #[props(optional)]
239	    pub dioxus_platform: Option<Arc<dyn DioxusPlatform>>,
240	    pub children: Element,
241	}
242	
243	impl PartialEq for ArsProviderSketchProps {
244	    fn eq(&self, other: &Self) -> bool;
245	}
246	
247	#[component]
248	pub fn ArsProvider(props: ArsProviderSketchProps) -> Element {
249	    let locale = props.locale.unwrap_or_else(|| {
250	        use_signal(|| Locale::parse("en-US").expect("en-US is always valid"))
251	    });
252	    let direction = use_memo(move || {
253	        props.direction
254	            .map(|d| d())
255	            .unwrap_or_else(|| locale.read().direction())
256	    });
257	    let color_mode = props.color_mode.unwrap_or_else(|| use_signal(|| ColorMode::System));
258	    let disabled = props.disabled.unwrap_or_else(|| use_signal(|| false));
259	    let read_only = props.read_only.unwrap_or_else(|| use_signal(|| false));
260	    let platform = props.platform.unwrap_or_else(|| resolve_platform_for_target());
261	    let intl_backend = props.intl_backend.unwrap_or_else(|| Arc::new(StubIntlBackend));
262	    let i18n_registries = props.i18n_registries.unwrap_or_else(|| Rc::new(I18nRegistries::new()));
263	    let style_strategy = props.style_strategy.unwrap_or(StyleStrategy::Inline);
264	    let dioxus_platform = props.dioxus_platform.unwrap_or_else(|| {
265	        #[cfg(feature = "web")]
266	        { Rc::new(WebPlatform) }
267	        #[cfg(all(feature = "desktop", not(feature = "web")))]
268	        { Rc::new(DesktopPlatform) }
269	        #[cfg(not(any(feature = "web", feature = "desktop")))]
270	        { Rc::new(NullPlatform) }
271	    });
272	
273	    let nonce_rules = use_signal(|| Vec::<NonceCssRule>::new());
274	    use_context_provider(|| ArsNonceCssCtx { rules: nonce_rules });
275	
276	    use_context_provider(|| ArsContext {
277	        locale,
278	        direction,
279	        color_mode,
280	        disabled,
281	        read_only,
282	        id_prefix: use_signal(|| props.id_prefix),
283	        portal_container_id: use_signal(|| props.portal_container_id),
284	        root_node_id: use_signal(|| props.root_node_id),
285	        platform,
286	        intl_backend,
287	        i18n_registries,
288	        style_strategy,
289	        dioxus_platform,
290	    });
291	
292	    let dir = use_memo(move || direction().as_html_attr().to_string());
293	
294	    rsx! {
295	        div { dir: "{dir}", {props.children} }
296	    }
297	}
298	```
299	
300	## 25. Adapter Invariants
301	
302	- Context publication and consumption rules must remain explicit so descendants know when environment data is available.
303	- The `dir` attribute on the wrapper MUST reactively update when direction changes.
304	- `use_locale()` MUST always return a valid signal, even without a provider.
305	- Dioxus SSR MUST NOT require a live platform context — ArsProvider publishes configuration only.
306	
307	## 26. Accessibility and SSR Notes
308	
309	The `<div dir>` wrapper propagates reading direction for correct BiDi layout. No ARIA semantics. SSR renders the wrapper with initial direction value. No platform lookup is needed during SSR.
310	
311	## 27. Parity Summary and Intentional Deviations
312	
313	Parity summary: full core context parity.
314	
315	Intentional deviations:
316	
317	- `dioxus_platform` is an adapter-only extra prop with no core equivalent. It provides Dioxus-specific platform services via the `DioxusPlatform` trait (§6 in `09-adapter-dioxus.md`).
318	
319	## 28. Test Scenarios
320	
321	- context publication with all props
322	- nonce collector publication for `ArsNonceStyle`
323	- default fallback values when props are absent
324	- `use_locale()` fallback without provider
325	- `dir` attribute reactivity on locale change
326	- SSR rendering on all Dioxus targets (web, desktop, mobile)
327	- `style_strategy` defaults to `StyleStrategy::Inline` when absent
328	- `dioxus_platform` resolves via feature flags when absent
329	
330	## 29. Test Oracle Notes
331	
332	| Behavior                | Preferred oracle type | Notes                                                                                |
333	| ----------------------- | --------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
334	| provider publication    | context registration  | Assert descendants observe the published ArsContext.                                 |
335	| locale fallback         | context registration  | Assert `use_locale()` returns en-US without a provider.                              |
336	| dir attribute           | DOM assertion         | Assert `dir` attribute matches current direction.                                    |
337	| SSR platform fallback   | context registration  | Verify context is published without platform lookup during SSR.                      |
338	| style strategy default  | context registration  | Assert `use_style_strategy()` returns `Inline` without explicit prop.                |
339	| dioxus platform default | context registration  | Assert `use_platform()` returns feature-flag-appropriate impl without explicit prop. |
340	| nonce collector         | context registration  | Assert `append_nonce_css()` collects rules from provider context.                    |
341	
342	## 30. Implementation Checklist
343	
344	- [ ] Provider context publishes all documented `ArsContext` fields.
345	- [ ] Provider publishes an `ArsNonceCssCtx` collector for `ArsNonceStyle`.
346	- [ ] `<div dir>` wrapper renders and reactively updates.
347	- [ ] Default fallback values match documented defaults (en-US, Ltr, System, false, false, Inline).
348	- [ ] `use_locale()` works with and without provider.
349	- [ ] SSR renders without platform lookup.
350	- [ ] `style_strategy` defaults to `StyleStrategy::Inline`.
351	- [ ] `dioxus_platform` defaults via feature flag resolution (Web > Desktop > NullPlatform).
352	- [ ] Context-registration test oracles are covered.
353

# ArsProvider — Dioxus Adapter

## 1. Purpose and Adapter Scope

This spec maps the core [`ArsProvider`](../../components/utility/ars-provider.md) context contract to Dioxus 0.7.x. `ArsProvider` is the single root provider — it subsumes the formerly separate `LocaleProvider`, `ArsStyleProvider`, and `PlatformProvider`.

## 2. Public Adapter API

```rust,no_check
#[derive(Props, Clone)]
pub struct ArsProviderProps {
    #[props(optional, into)]
    pub locale: Option<Signal<Locale>>,
    #[props(optional, into)]
    pub direction: Option<Signal<Direction>>,
    #[props(optional, into)]
    pub color_mode: Option<Signal<ColorMode>>,
    #[props(optional, into)]
    pub disabled: Option<Signal<bool>>,
    #[props(optional, into)]
    pub read_only: Option<Signal<bool>>,
    #[props(optional)]
    pub id_prefix: Option<String>,
    #[props(optional)]
    pub portal_container_id: Option<String>,
    #[props(optional)]
    pub root_node_id: Option<String>,
    #[props(optional)]
    pub platform: Option<Arc<dyn PlatformEffects>>,
    #[props(optional)]
    pub intl_backend: Option<Arc<dyn IntlBackend>>,
    #[props(optional)]
    pub i18n_registries: Option<Arc<I18nRegistries>>,
    #[props(optional)]
    pub style_strategy: Option<StyleStrategy>,
    #[props(optional)]
    pub dioxus_platform: Option<Arc<dyn DioxusPlatform>>,
    pub children: Element,
}

impl PartialEq for ArsProviderProps {
    fn eq(&self, other: &Self) -> bool;
}

#[component]
pub fn ArsProvider(props: ArsProviderProps) -> Element
```

## 3. Mapping to Core Component Contract

- Props parity: full parity with the core context props. Reactive props (`locale`, `direction`, `color_mode`, `disabled`, `read_only`) accept `Signal` for live updates. Non-reactive props (`id_prefix`, `portal_container_id`, `root_node_id`) are plain values set at mount time.
- `style_strategy` maps directly to the core `StyleStrategy` prop (defaults to `StyleStrategy::Inline`).
- `dioxus_platform` is an adapter-only extra prop (no core equivalent) — it provides Dioxus-specific platform services (file pickers, clipboard, drag data).
- `ArsProviderProps` must implement `Clone + PartialEq`, but `PartialEq` may be implemented manually so trait-object fields can use adapter-defined semantic equality.
- Context parity: publishes full `ArsContext` and the adapter nonce CSS collector to descendants via `use_context_provider`.

## 4. Part Mapping

| Core part / structure | Required?     | Adapter rendering target | Ownership      | Attr source | Notes                                 |
| --------------------- | ------------- | ------------------------ | -------------- | ----------- | ------------------------------------- |
| ars provider boundary | provider-only | `<div dir="{dir}">`      | adapter-owned  | direction   | Wrapper propagates `dir` attribute.   |
| children subtree      | required      | consumer children        | consumer-owned | none        | Descendants consume provided context. |

## 5. Attr Merge and Ownership Rules

| Target node     | Core attrs | Adapter-owned attrs            | Consumer attrs | Merge order    | Ownership notes                                |
| --------------- | ---------- | ------------------------------ | -------------- | -------------- | ---------------------------------------------- |
| `<div>` wrapper | none       | `dir` (derived from direction) | none           | not applicable | Minimal wrapper for BiDi direction propagation |

## 6. Composition / Context Contract

The adapter publishes `ArsContext` with `use_context_provider`. It also publishes an `ArsNonceCssCtx` collector owned by the provider instance and automatically renders `ArsNonceStyle` when `style_strategy` is `StyleStrategy::Nonce(nonce)`. Consumers use `try_use_context::<ArsContext>()`. The convenience hooks `use_locale()` and `use_direction()` read from this context with fallback defaults. During Dioxus SSR, all context values are platform-agnostic and require no browser globals.

## 7. Prop Sync and Event Mapping

ArsProvider is context-driven rather than event-driven.

| Adapter prop                                         | Mode       | Sync trigger            | Update path                       | Visible effect                               | Notes                                                                     |
| ---------------------------------------------------- | ---------- | ----------------------- | --------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `locale`                                             | controlled | signal change           | update ArsContext.locale          | descendants see updated locale               | direction auto-inferred when direction=None                               |
| `direction`                                          | controlled | signal change           | update ArsContext.direction       | `dir` attribute updates, layout reflows      | overrides locale-inferred direction                                       |
| `color_mode`                                         | controlled | signal change           | update ArsContext.color_mode      | theme-aware descendants re-render            |                                                                           |
| `disabled` / `read_only`                             | controlled | signal change           | update ArsContext fields          | descendant components enable/disable         |                                                                           |
| `id_prefix` / `portal_container_id` / `root_node_id` | controlled | prop change after mount | update provided context           | descendants resolve new ID prefix/targets    | non-reactive; set once at mount                                           |
| `style_strategy`                                     | controlled | prop set at mount       | update ArsContext.style_strategy  | descendant components use strategy for CSS   | defaults to `StyleStrategy::Inline`; `Nonce` auto-renders the nonce style |
| `dioxus_platform`                                    | controlled | prop set at mount       | update ArsContext.dioxus_platform | adapter-specific platform services available | defaults via feature flag resolution                                      |
| nonce CSS collector                                  | internal   | provider mount          | provide `ArsNonceCssCtx`          | provider-owned style renders rules           | provider-owned                                                            |

## 8. Registration and Cleanup Contract

- No DOM registration lifecycle beyond the `<div>` wrapper.
- Cleanup is the lifetime of the provided context value only.
- Descendants must stop reading provider data after the provider unmounts.

| Registered entity   | Registration trigger | Identity key      | Cleanup trigger  | Cleanup action     | Notes                                             |
| ------------------- | -------------------- | ----------------- | ---------------- | ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------- |
| provided ArsContext | provider mount       | provider instance | provider cleanup | drop context value | no DOM listener ownership in the provider itself  |
| nonce CSS collector | provider mount       | provider instance | provider cleanup | drop context value | rendered automatically for `StyleStrategy::Nonce` |

## 9. Ref and Node Contract

| Target part / node | Ref required? | Ref owner      | Node availability | Composition rule | Notes                                             |
| ------------------ | ------------- | -------------- | ----------------- | ---------------- | ------------------------------------------------- |
| `<div>` wrapper    | no            | not applicable | server-safe       | not applicable   | Renders a minimal `dir` wrapper on all platforms. |

## 10. State Machine Boundary Rules

- machine-owned state: provided context values exposed through `ArsContext`.
- adapter-local derived bookkeeping: `Memo<Direction>` derived from locale signal when direction prop is `None`.
- forbidden local mirrors: do not keep separate unsynchronized copies outside the published context.
- allowed snapshot-read contexts: provider render/effects only.

## 11. Callback Payload Contract

| Callback           | Payload source | Payload shape | Timing         | Cancelable? | Notes                                                      |
| ------------------ | -------------- | ------------- | -------------- | ----------- | ---------------------------------------------------------- |
| no public callback | none           | none          | not applicable | no          | Descendants consume context rather than callback payloads. |

## 12. Failure and Degradation Rules

| Condition                                                   | Policy             | Notes                                                                                  |
| ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| locale signal absent (None prop)                            | use default        | Default to `en-US`, direction inferred as `Ltr`.                                       |
| descendant expects required environment data that is absent | warn and ignore    | The provider remains valid; required-descendant behavior belongs to the consumer spec. |
| platform unavailable during SSR or server-only rendering    | degrade gracefully | All context values are platform-agnostic; no platform lookup needed.                   |
| `style_strategy` absent                                     | use default        | Default to `StyleStrategy::Inline`.                                                    |
| `dioxus_platform` absent                                    | use default        | Resolved via feature flags: Web > Desktop > NullPlatform.                              |

## 13. Identity and Key Policy

| Registered or repeated structure | Identity source  | Duplicates allowed? | DOM order must match registration order? | SSR/hydration stability                                        | Notes                                       |
| -------------------------------- | ---------------- | ------------------- | ---------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------- |
| provided ArsContext              | instance-derived | not applicable      | not applicable                           | provider instance identity must remain stable across hydration | Provider lifetime is the identity boundary. |

## 14. SSR and Client Boundary Rules

- The provider renders a `<div dir>` wrapper during SSR with the initial direction value.
- All context values are platform-agnostic (no browser dependencies in the context itself).
- Platform-specific operations go through `PlatformEffects` or `DioxusPlatform`, not through ArsProvider.
- On server rendering, no platform lookup is required — ArsProvider publishes plain configuration values.

## 15. Performance Constraints

- Provided context should only update when incoming signal values actually change.
- The `direction` memo only recomputes when locale or direction prop changes.
- Chain `dir` attribute off the `direction` memo to avoid redundant re-reads of `locale`.

## 16. Implementation Dependencies

| Dependency    | Required?   | Dependency type     | Why it must exist first                                                             | Notes                                                                                       |
| ------------- | ----------- | ------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `dismissable` | recommended | downstream consumer | Dismissable-like utilities often consume environment context for scoped DOM access. | Dependency is about implementation order in the wider utility layer, not runtime ownership. |
| `focus-scope` | recommended | downstream consumer | Focus utilities may rely on scoped environment data in composite trees.             | Same guidance applies to future overlay utilities.                                          |

## 17. Recommended Implementation Sequence

1. Resolve all props to signals (defaulting absent signals to stored defaults).
2. Derive `direction` memo from locale when direction prop is `None`.
3. Resolve `style_strategy` (default `StyleStrategy::Inline`).
4. Resolve `dioxus_platform` via feature flag fallback (Web > Desktop > NullPlatform).
5. Build and publish provider-owned `ArsNonceCssCtx`.
6. Build and publish `ArsContext` via `use_context_provider`.
7. Render `div { dir: "{dir}", {children} }`.
8. Verify descendant consumption and provider cleanup.

## 18. Anti-Patterns

- Do not fail hard when optional props are absent — use documented defaults.
- Do not add heavy DOM structure beyond the `<div dir>` wrapper.
- Do not attempt platform lookup (e.g., `use_platform()`) inside ArsProvider — it provides configuration, not platform capabilities.

## 19. Consumer Expectations and Guarantees

- Consumers may assume `ArsContext` is always present when rendered inside an `ArsProvider`.
- Consumers may assume `use_locale()` returns a valid locale signal even without a provider (fallback to `en-US`).
- Consumers may assume the `dir` attribute on the wrapper reflects the current direction.

## 20. Platform Support Matrix

| Capability / behavior   | Web          | Desktop      | Mobile       | SSR          | Notes                                      |
| ----------------------- | ------------ | ------------ | ------------ | ------------ | ------------------------------------------ |
| context publication     | full support | full support | full support | full support | All context values are platform-agnostic.  |
| `dir` wrapper rendering | full support | full support | full support | full support | Renders `<div dir>` on all Dioxus targets. |

## 21. Debug Diagnostics and Production Policy

| Condition                   | Debug build behavior | Production behavior | Notes                                    |
| --------------------------- | -------------------- | ------------------- | ---------------------------------------- |
| no ArsProvider in ancestors | debug warning        | degrade gracefully  | `use_locale()` returns fallback `en-US`. |

## 22. Shared Adapter Helper Notes

| Helper concept      | Required? | Responsibility                                  | Reused by                       | Notes                                   |
| ------------------- | --------- | ----------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------- | --------------------------------------- |
| `use_locale()` hook | required  | Read locale from ArsContext with en-US fallback | all locale-dependent components | Defined in `09-adapter-dioxus.md` §16.1 |

## 23. Framework-Specific Behavior

Dioxus targets web, desktop, and mobile. `ArsContext` bundles platform-agnostic configuration, the `PlatformEffects` trait object, and the Dioxus-specific `DioxusPlatform` trait object. The `dioxus_platform` prop is an adapter-only extra with no core equivalent — it provides Dioxus-specific platform services (file pickers, clipboard, drag data) not covered by `PlatformEffects`. The Dioxus adapter resolves the platform implementation via feature flags: `WebPlatform` (web), `DesktopPlatform` (desktop), `NullPlatform` (SSR/tests/mobile fallback). The `style_strategy` field controls CSS injection for all descendant ars components.

## 24. Canonical Implementation Sketch

```rust
#[derive(Props, Clone)]
pub struct ArsProviderSketchProps {
    #[props(optional, into)]
    pub locale: Option<Signal<Locale>>,
    #[props(optional, into)]
    pub direction: Option<Signal<Direction>>,
    #[props(optional, into)]
    pub color_mode: Option<Signal<ColorMode>>,
    #[props(optional, into)]
    pub disabled: Option<Signal<bool>>,
    #[props(optional, into)]
    pub read_only: Option<Signal<bool>>,
    #[props(optional)]
    pub id_prefix: Option<String>,
    #[props(optional)]
    pub portal_container_id: Option<String>,
    #[props(optional)]
    pub root_node_id: Option<String>,
    #[props(optional)]
    pub platform: Option<Arc<dyn PlatformEffects>>,
    #[props(optional)]
    pub intl_backend: Option<Arc<dyn IntlBackend>>,
    #[props(optional)]
    pub i18n_registries: Option<Arc<I18nRegistries>>,
    #[props(optional)]
    pub style_strategy: Option<StyleStrategy>,
    #[props(optional)]
    pub dioxus_platform: Option<Arc<dyn DioxusPlatform>>,
    pub children: Element,
}

impl PartialEq for ArsProviderSketchProps {
    fn eq(&self, other: &Self) -> bool;
}

#[component]
pub fn ArsProvider(props: ArsProviderSketchProps) -> Element {
    let locale = props.locale.unwrap_or_else(|| {
        use_signal(|| Locale::parse("en-US").expect("en-US is always valid"))
    });
    let direction = use_memo(move || {
        props.direction
            .map(|d| d())
            .unwrap_or_else(|| locale.read().direction())
    });
    let color_mode = props.color_mode.unwrap_or_else(|| use_signal(|| ColorMode::System));
    let disabled = props.disabled.unwrap_or_else(|| use_signal(|| false));
    let read_only = props.read_only.unwrap_or_else(|| use_signal(|| false));
    let platform = props.platform.unwrap_or_else(|| resolve_platform_for_target());
    let intl_backend = props.intl_backend.unwrap_or_else(|| Arc::new(StubIntlBackend));
    let i18n_registries = props.i18n_registries.unwrap_or_else(|| Rc::new(I18nRegistries::new()));
    let style_strategy = props.style_strategy.unwrap_or(StyleStrategy::Inline);
    let dioxus_platform = props.dioxus_platform.unwrap_or_else(|| {
        #[cfg(feature = "web")]
        { Rc::new(WebPlatform) }
        #[cfg(all(feature = "desktop", not(feature = "web")))]
        { Rc::new(DesktopPlatform) }
        #[cfg(not(any(feature = "web", feature = "desktop")))]
        { Rc::new(NullPlatform) }
    });

    let nonce_rules = use_signal(|| Vec::<NonceCssRule>::new());
    use_context_provider(|| ArsNonceCssCtx { rules: nonce_rules });

    use_context_provider(|| ArsContext {
        locale,
        direction,
        color_mode,
        disabled,
        read_only,
        id_prefix: use_signal(|| props.id_prefix),
        portal_container_id: use_signal(|| props.portal_container_id),
        root_node_id: use_signal(|| props.root_node_id),
        platform,
        intl_backend,
        i18n_registries,
        style_strategy,
        dioxus_platform,
    });

    let dir = use_memo(move || direction().as_html_attr().to_string());

    rsx! {
        div { dir: "{dir}", {props.children} }
    }
}
```

## 25. Adapter Invariants

- Context publication and consumption rules must remain explicit so descendants know when environment data is available.
- The `dir` attribute on the wrapper MUST reactively update when direction changes.
- `use_locale()` MUST always return a valid signal, even without a provider.
- Dioxus SSR MUST NOT require a live platform context — ArsProvider publishes configuration only.

## 26. Accessibility and SSR Notes

The `<div dir>` wrapper propagates reading direction for correct BiDi layout. No ARIA semantics. SSR renders the wrapper with initial direction value. No platform lookup is needed during SSR.

## 27. Parity Summary and Intentional Deviations

Parity summary: full core context parity.

Intentional deviations:

- `dioxus_platform` is an adapter-only extra prop with no core equivalent. It provides Dioxus-specific platform services via the `DioxusPlatform` trait (§6 in `09-adapter-dioxus.md`).

## 28. Test Scenarios

- context publication with all props
- nonce collector publication for `ArsNonceStyle`
- default fallback values when props are absent
- `use_locale()` fallback without provider
- `dir` attribute reactivity on locale change
- SSR rendering on all Dioxus targets (web, desktop, mobile)
- `style_strategy` defaults to `StyleStrategy::Inline` when absent
- `dioxus_platform` resolves via feature flags when absent

## 29. Test Oracle Notes

| Behavior                | Preferred oracle type | Notes                                                                                |
| ----------------------- | --------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| provider publication    | context registration  | Assert descendants observe the published ArsContext.                                 |
| locale fallback         | context registration  | Assert `use_locale()` returns en-US without a provider.                              |
| dir attribute           | DOM assertion         | Assert `dir` attribute matches current direction.                                    |
| SSR platform fallback   | context registration  | Verify context is published without platform lookup during SSR.                      |
| style strategy default  | context registration  | Assert `use_style_strategy()` returns `Inline` without explicit prop.                |
| dioxus platform default | context registration  | Assert `use_platform()` returns feature-flag-appropriate impl without explicit prop. |
| nonce collector         | context registration  | Assert `append_nonce_css()` collects rules from provider context.                    |

## 30. Implementation Checklist

- [ ] Provider context publishes all documented `ArsContext` fields.
- [ ] Provider publishes an `ArsNonceCssCtx` collector for `ArsNonceStyle`.
- [ ] `<div dir>` wrapper renders and reactively updates.
- [ ] Default fallback values match documented defaults (en-US, Ltr, System, false, false, Inline).
- [ ] `use_locale()` works with and without provider.
- [ ] SSR renders without platform lookup.
- [ ] `style_strategy` defaults to `StyleStrategy::Inline`.
- [ ] `dioxus_platform` defaults via feature flag resolution (Web > Desktop > NullPlatform).
- [ ] Context-registration test oracles are covered.

# Spec Directory Guide

## File Conventions

- `_category.md` files contain category overviews and shared conventions
- Component files use kebab-case matching the component name
- Shared types in `shared/` are referenced by component frontmatter
- Headings are numbered (`## 1. State Machine`, `### 1.1 States`)

## Spec Changes During Implementation

The repo is in active implementation. Spec edits inside `spec/` should usually be tied to a concrete GitHub implementation task or PR, not made as detached cleanup.

- Read the implementation issue before changing spec text.
- Keep cited spec sections synchronized with the implementation task that discovered the mismatch.
- If an adapter-specific implementation exposes a missing shared abstraction, promote that abstraction into the appropriate foundation/shared spec instead of documenting it only in framework code.
- Do not leave placeholder notes for later reconciliation; resolve the spec/code mismatch in the same task whenever possible.

## Adding a New Component

1. Create `components/{category}/{component-name}.md` with YAML frontmatter
2. Follow the section structure defined in `foundation/10-component-spec-template.md` for the appropriate tier
3. Add an entry to `manifest.toml` with path, category, deps, and related
4. Update `foundation/02-component-catalog.md` with the new component
5. Run `cargo run -p spec-tool -- validate` to verify consistency

## Component File Format

```markdown
---
component: ComponentName
category: { category }
tier: stateless | stateful | complex
foundation_deps: [architecture, accessibility, ...]
shared_deps: [date-time-types, ...]
related: [sibling-component, ...]
---

# ComponentName
```

See `foundation/10-component-spec-template.md` for the canonical section structure, ordering rules, conformance checklist, and skeleton examples for each tier.

152	pub fn ArsProvider(props: ArsProviderProps) -> Element {
153	    let ArsProviderProps {
154	        locale,
155	        direction,
156	        color_mode,
157	        disabled,
158	        read_only,
159	        id_prefix,
160	        portal_container_id,
161	        root_node_id,
162	        platform,
163	        intl_backend,
164	        i18n_registries,
165	        style_strategy,
166	        dioxus_platform,
167	        children,
168	    } = props;
169	    let fallback_locale = use_signal(locales::en_us);
170	    let locale = locale.unwrap_or(fallback_locale);
171	
172	    let explicit_direction = direction;
173	
174	    let direction = use_memo(move || {
175	        explicit_direction.map_or_else(
176	            || direction_from_locale(&locale.read()),
177	            |signal| *signal.read(),
178	        )
179	    });
180	
181	    let fallback_color_mode = use_signal(|| ColorMode::System);
182	    let color_mode = color_mode.unwrap_or(fallback_color_mode);
183	
184	    let fallback_disabled = use_signal(|| false);
185	    let disabled = disabled.unwrap_or(fallback_disabled);
186	
187	    let fallback_read_only = use_signal(|| false);
188	    let read_only = read_only.unwrap_or(fallback_read_only);
189	
190	    let direction_for_context = direction;
191	
192	    let direction_for_render = direction;
193	
194	    use_nonce_css_context_provider();
195	
196	    #[cfg(all(feature = "web", target_arch = "wasm32"))]
197	    let is_root_provider = try_use_context::<ArsContext>().is_none();
198	
199	    let style_strategy = style_strategy.unwrap_or(StyleStrategy::Inline);
200	    let nonce = match &style_strategy {
201	        StyleStrategy::Nonce(nonce) => Some(nonce.clone()),
202	        StyleStrategy::Inline | StyleStrategy::Cssom => None,
203	    };
204	
205	    use_context_provider(move || ArsContext {
206	        locale,
207	        direction: direction_for_context,
208	        color_mode,
209	        disabled,
210	        read_only,
211	        id_prefix: Signal::new(id_prefix),
212	        portal_container_id: Signal::new(portal_container_id),
213	        root_node_id: Signal::new(root_node_id),
214	        platform: platform.unwrap_or_else(|| Arc::new(NullPlatformEffects)),
215	        modality: Arc::new(DefaultModalityContext::new()),
216	        intl_backend: intl_backend.unwrap_or_else(|| Arc::new(StubIntlBackend)),
217	        i18n_registries: i18n_registries.unwrap_or_else(|| Arc::new(I18nRegistries::new())),
218	        dioxus_platform: dioxus_platform.unwrap_or_else(default_dioxus_platform),
219	        style_strategy,
220	    });
221	
222	    let dir = direction_for_render.read().as_html_attr();
223	
224	    #[cfg(all(feature = "web", target_arch = "wasm32"))]
225	    use_effect(move || {
226	        if is_root_provider {
227	            crate::hydration::mark_body_hydrated();
228	        }
229	    });
230	
231	    rsx! {
232	        div { dir,
233	            if let Some(nonce) = nonce {
234	                ArsNonceStyle { nonce }
235	            }
236	            {children}
237	        }
238	    }
239	}

content.content:
1	//! Convenience re-exports for end users of the Leptos adapter.
2	//!
3	//! A single `use ars_leptos::prelude::*;` gives application authors access to
4	//! the components, user-facing traits, and configuration types they need —
5	//! without hunting through individual `ars_*` crates.
6	//!
7	//! # What belongs in the prelude
8	//!
9	//! Only items that **end users** interact with directly:
10	//!
11	//! 1. **Component modules** — as components land (e.g., `button`, `dialog`,
12	//!    `select`), their public module paths are re-exported here so users can
13	//!    write `button::Props`, `dialog::Machine`, etc.
14	//!
15	//! 2. **User-facing traits** — traits that end users call on component outputs
16	//!    (e.g., `Translate` from `ars-i18n` for localised strings). Re-exporting
17	//!    the trait avoids forcing users to add a direct dependency on the
18	//!    subsystem crate just to call a method.
19	//!
20	//! 3. **Configuration types** — types that appear in component props or that
21	//!    users pass to configure behaviour (e.g., `Locale`, `Direction`,
22	//!    `Orientation`, `Selection`).
23	//!
24	//! # What does NOT belong in the prelude
25	//!
26	//! - **Core engine internals** — `Machine`, `Service`, `ConnectApi`,
27	//!   `ComponentPart`, `AttrMap`, `Bindable`, `TransitionPlan`, `PendingEffect`.
28	//!   These are implementation details used by component authors *inside* the
29	//!   adapter crate, not by end users consuming components.
30	//! - **Accessibility primitives** — `AriaRole`, `AriaAttribute`, `ComponentIds`.
31	//!   Wired internally by each component; end users never construct these.
32	//! - **Interaction primitives** — `merge_attrs`, `PressState`, `FocusState`.
33	//!   Internal to component implementations.
34	//! - **Adapter hooks** — `use_machine`, `UseMachineReturn`, `EphemeralRef`.
35	//!   Used by component modules inside the adapter crate, not by end users
36	//!   who consume the ready-made components. (Still publicly accessible via
37	//!   `ars_leptos::use_machine` for advanced users building custom machines.)
38	//! - **Framework re-exports** — `leptos::prelude::*` should be imported
39	//!   separately; we do not re-export Leptos types to avoid version coupling.
40	//!
41	//! # Growth policy
42	//!
43	//! When adding a new item, ask: "Does an end user writing `<Button>` or
44	//! `<Dialog>` in their app need this?" If yes, add it. If only component
45	//! implementors inside this crate need it, keep it as a regular import.
46	
47	// -- User-facing traits --
48	pub use ars_collections::{Key, TabKey};
49	pub use ars_core::{I18nRegistries, MessageFn, MessagesRegistry, SafeUrl, UnsafeUrlError};
50	pub use ars_i18n::{Direction, IntlBackend, Locale, Orientation, ResolvedDirection, Translate};
51	
52	// -- Component modules --
53	//
54	// Consumers reach component types via the module qualifier
55	// (e.g. `dismissable::Props`, `dismissable::Region`, `dismissable::Handle`,
56	// `dismissable::DismissReason`, `dismissable::use_dismissable`); never via
57	// flattened aliases such as `DismissableProps` or `DismissableRegion`.
58	//
59	// The adapter `dismissable` module re-exports the agnostic
60	// `ars_components::utility::dismissable::*` surface, so this single
61	// re-export covers both the framework-agnostic types (`Props`, `Messages`,
62	// `DismissReason`, …) and the Leptos-side wrappers (`Handle`, `Region`,
63	// `RegionProps`, `use_dismissable`).
64	pub use crate::as_child;
65	pub use crate::navigation::{self, tabs};
66	// The `error_boundary` adapter module exposes the `ArsErrorBoundary`
67	// wrapper component spec'd at
68	// `spec/foundation/08-adapter-leptos.md` §17. End users reach it as
69	// `error_boundary::ArsErrorBoundary` after `use ars_leptos::prelude::*;`.
70	pub use crate::utility::{
71	    self, button, client_only, dismissable, error_boundary, separator, visually_hidden,
72	    z_index_allocator,
73	};
74	// -- User-facing helpers --
75	pub use crate::{Translatable, t, use_number_formatter};
76

content.text: I have enough context to plan now. Let me write the plan file.

input.content:
# Plan: Finish ArsProvider Leptos (#347) + Dioxus (#477) adapters

## Context

The two adapter tasks ship the **ArsProvider** component shell — the single root context provider for every ars-ui app (locale, direction, color mode, disabled/read-only, ID prefix, portal/root nodes, platform effects, intl backend, i18n registries, style strategy, plus `dioxus_platform` on Dioxus). The agnostic core (`ArsContext`, `ColorMode`, `PlatformEffects` in `ars-core`) landed via #213; the reactive shell, hooks, and context publishing landed via #190 (Leptos) and #193 (Dioxus). #477 was then closed as superseded by #193, but the discrete *adapter component* deliverable for both frameworks was never finished:

- No dedicated `tests/ars_provider*.rs` integration tests in either adapter crate.
- `ArsProvider` is not exposed in either adapter's `prelude` — every other delivered component module is.
- Spec §28 mandates oracles for SSR rendering, nonce auto-render when `StyleStrategy::Nonce`, default `StyleStrategy::Inline`, and (Dioxus) feature-flag-based `dioxus_platform` defaults — none of these are exercised end-to-end through the public component.

The component implementations themselves (`crates/ars-leptos/src/provider.rs` lines 169-254, `crates/ars-dioxus/src/provider.rs` lines 28-239) and ~40 inline unit/wasm tests already exist; this PR adds the missing integration-tier surface so #347 and #477 can close against a green CI + Codex 👍.

## Scope

One bundled PR closing **#347** and **#477** (reopen #477 first). Add adapter-tier integration test files, expose `ArsProvider` in both preludes, fill the spec §28 gaps. No new dependencies. No spec changes (specs already authoritative). No changes to the component implementations or inline tests.

## Reference: agnostic + adapter surface

- Core: `crates/ars-core/src/provider.rs` (`ArsContext`, `ColorMode::{Light, Dark, System}`), `crates/ars-core/src/platform.rs` (`NullPlatformEffects`).
- Leptos component + hooks: `crates/ars-leptos/src/provider.rs` (`ArsProvider`, `ArsContext`, `use_locale`, `use_direction`, `use_style_strategy` at `attrs.rs:1226`, `use_intl_backend`, `use_modality_context`, `use_platform_effects`, `use_number_formatter`, `t`).
- Dioxus component + hooks: `crates/ars-dioxus/src/provider.rs` (same surface + `ArsProviderProps`, `arc_option_ptr_eq`, `default_dioxus_platform`).
- Nonce: `crates/ars-leptos/src/nonce.rs` and `crates/ars-dioxus/src/nonce.rs` (`ArsNonceCssCtx`, `ArsNonceStyle`, `use_nonce_css_context_provider`, `NonceCssRule`, `append_nonce_css`).
- SSR oracle pattern: `crates/ars-leptos/tests/client_only.rs` (Leptos `view!{}.to_html()`), `crates/ars-dioxus/tests/client_only.rs` (Dioxus `VirtualDom::new(app); vdom.rebuild_in_place(); dioxus_ssr::render(&vdom)`).
- WASM probe pattern: existing inline `ProviderProbe` / `ConfiguredProviderProbe` in each adapter's `provider.rs` test module — reuse the **shape** (don't import; tests/ files can't see `pub(crate)` helpers, so define a local probe).

## Files to modify

### Leptos (#347)

1. **Create** `crates/ars-leptos/tests/ars_provider.rs` — native SSR oracle, gated `#![cfg(all(not(target_arch = "wasm32"), feature = "ssr"))]`, mirroring the `client_only.rs` shape. Scenarios:
   - Renders `<div dir="ltr">` wrapper with children for default props (no locale supplied).
   - Renders `<div dir="rtl">` when an RTL locale (e.g. `Locale::parse("ar-EG")`) is provided.
   - Renders `<div dir="rtl">` when explicit `direction=Signal::stored(Direction::Rtl)` overrides an LTR locale.
   - When `style_strategy=StyleStrategy::Nonce("test-nonce")`, the SSR HTML contains a `<style nonce="test-nonce">` tag (sourced from `ArsNonceStyle`).
   - When `style_strategy=StyleStrategy::Inline` (default), no `<style nonce="…">` is emitted.

2. **Create** `crates/ars-leptos/tests/ars_provider_wasm.rs` — `#![cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]` + `wasm_bindgen_test_configure!(run_in_browser)`. Use a locally-defined `#[component] fn Probe()` that reads `use_context::<ArsContext>()` (reuse the pattern from the existing inline `ConfiguredProviderProbe`). Scenarios that complement (don't duplicate) the inline tests:
   - All-props configuration round-trip: `locale`, `direction` override, `color_mode`, `disabled`, `read_only`, `id_prefix`, `portal_container_id`, `root_node_id`, `style_strategy=Cssom` — every field observable via the probe matches the input.
   - Nonce auto-render: when `style_strategy=StyleStrategy::Nonce(...)` is passed, a probe verifies (a) `ArsNonceCssCtx` is published and (b) the rendered DOM contains a `<style>` element with the expected nonce attribute (mount via `leptos::mount::mount_to(...)` then query).
   - `use_style_strategy()` fallback: outside any provider, returns `StyleStrategy::Inline`.

3. **Edit** `crates/ars-leptos/src/prelude.rs` — add `pub use crate::ArsProvider;` and `pub use ars_core::ColorMode;` (color mode is a configuration type users pass via the `color_mode` prop; add a short doc comment on the `ColorMode` line to explain why it's in the prelude).

### Dioxus (#477)

4. **Create** `crates/ars-dioxus/tests/ars_provider.rs` — native SSR oracle, gated `#![cfg(not(target_arch = "wasm32"))]`, using the `render_app` helper from `client_only.rs`. Scenarios:
   - Default props render `<div dir="ltr">` with children.
   - RTL locale renders `<div dir="rtl">`.
   - Explicit `direction` prop overrides locale-derived direction.
   - `style_strategy=StyleStrategy::Nonce("test-nonce")` emits `<style nonce="test-nonce">`.
   - Default (`None`) `style_strategy` emits no nonce style.
   - **Dioxus-specific:** when `dioxus_platform` is `None`, the published context's `dioxus_platform` resolves to `default_dioxus_platform()` (verified via a probe component reading `use_context::<ArsContext>()`, since this is a context-registration oracle per spec §29).

5. **Create** `crates/ars-dioxus/tests/ars_provider_wasm.rs` — `#![cfg(all(target_arch = "wasm32", feature = "web"))]` + `wasm_bindgen_test_configure!(run_in_browser)`. Scenarios analogous to Leptos wasm tests, plus:
   - `dioxus_platform` resolution when no prop supplied (context-registration oracle).
   - Nonce DOM presence verified via mount + querySelector.

6. **Edit** `crates/ars-dioxus/src/prelude.rs` — add `pub use crate::{ArsProvider, ArsProviderProps};` and `pub use ars_core::ColorMode;` symmetric to Leptos. (Dioxus needs the `Props` struct re-exported because explicit Props derives are part of the user-callable surface.)

### Both adapters

7. Run `post-implementation-audit` skill on the diff: spec drift pass (compare new tests against §28/§29 oracles + §30 checklist), iterative "anything missing?" pass (≥2 rounds), test-coverage audit (`cargo llvm-cov test -p ars-leptos --text -- ars_provider` and same for Dioxus; uncovered branches must be intentional). Land every audit finding in this PR — no follow-ups.

8. Reopen #477 (`gh issue reopen 477`) so the PR's `Closes #477` auto-close keyword works.

9. Open one PR targeting `main` with body containing `Closes #347` and `Closes #477`. Wait for codex review via the `waiting-for-codex-review` skill; address every thread.

## Spec parity notes (not changing the spec)

- Issue #347's "Tests to add first" template lists "ARIA attributes from core ConnectApi" and "Axe-core audit using `data-ars-scope` selector". Neither applies to `ArsProvider`: spec §1.2 documents it as a context-only provider with no ConnectApi/`Part`/`data-ars-scope` (the `<div dir>` wrapper has zero ARIA semantics by design — spec §26). The PR description should call this out explicitly so the reviewer doesn't expect those tests. Spec §28 is the authoritative test-scenarios list and is what we're delivering against.
- Leptos task description references "ZIndexAllocator and ClientOnly Leptos adapter" (#319) as a dependency — already closed and shipped. No action needed.

## Verification

Run from the worktree root (`/Users/ericson/.t3/worktrees/ars-ui/t3code-272d6151`):

1. **Targeted unit/integration:**
   - `cargo test -p ars-leptos --features ssr --test ars_provider`
   - `cargo test -p ars-dioxus --test ars_provider`
   - Wasm: `wasm-pack test --chrome --headless crates/ars-leptos --test ars_provider_wasm` (or use the workspace recipe used by existing `*_wasm` files; check `Cargo.toml` for the canonical command).
2. **Coverage probe (per crate, per CLAUDE.md):**
   - `cargo llvm-cov test -p ars-leptos --text -- ars_provider` — inspect uncovered branches in `provider.rs` lines around the `ArsProvider` component body; verify nonce + style strategy branches are now hit.
   - Same for `ars-dioxus`.
3. **Workspace gate before push:** `cargo xci-fast` (≈5 min, includes fmt, check, clippy, unit/integration/adapter, spec-compile-snippets, error-variant-coverage, snapshot-count).
4. **Codex review:** invoke `waiting-for-codex-review` skill after first push; loop until 👍.
5. **Manual eyeball on PR:** confirm `Closes #347` and `Closes #477` keywords are present; #477 reopened before PR opens; both preludes symmetric.

## Out of scope (explicitly)

- No changes to `ArsProvider` component implementation in either `provider.rs` — already spec-compliant.
- No refactoring of existing inline tests — they cover unit-level concerns and stay where they are.
- No new dependencies — no `Cargo.toml` edits.
- No spec edits — spec is authoritative and unchanged.
- `ArsContext` does not enter the prelude (it's a published context value, not a configuration type users pass in). Advanced users still reach it via `ars_leptos::ArsContext` / `ars_dioxus::ArsContext`.

input.plan:
# Plan: Finish ArsProvider Leptos (#347) + Dioxus (#477) adapters

## Context

The two adapter tasks ship the **ArsProvider** component shell — the single root context provider for every ars-ui app (locale, direction, color mode, disabled/read-only, ID prefix, portal/root nodes, platform effects, intl backend, i18n registries, style strategy, plus `dioxus_platform` on Dioxus). The agnostic core (`ArsContext`, `ColorMode`, `PlatformEffects` in `ars-core`) landed via #213; the reactive shell, hooks, and context publishing landed via #190 (Leptos) and #193 (Dioxus). #477 was then closed as superseded by #193, but the discrete *adapter component* deliverable for both frameworks was never finished:

- No dedicated `tests/ars_provider*.rs` integration tests in either adapter crate.
- `ArsProvider` is not exposed in either adapter's `prelude` — every other delivered component module is.
- Spec §28 mandates oracles for SSR rendering, nonce auto-render when `StyleStrategy::Nonce`, default `StyleStrategy::Inline`, and (Dioxus) feature-flag-based `dioxus_platform` defaults — none of these are exercised end-to-end through the public component.

The component implementations themselves (`crates/ars-leptos/src/provider.rs` lines 169-254, `crates/ars-dioxus/src/provider.rs` lines 28-239) and ~40 inline unit/wasm tests already exist; this PR adds the missing integration-tier surface so #347 and #477 can close against a green CI + Codex 👍.

## Scope

One bundled PR closing **#347** and **#477** (reopen #477 first). Add adapter-tier integration test files, expose `ArsProvider` in both preludes, fill the spec §28 gaps. No new dependencies. No spec changes (specs already authoritative). No changes to the component implementations or inline tests.

## Reference: agnostic + adapter surface

- Core: `crates/ars-core/src/provider.rs` (`ArsContext`, `ColorMode::{Light, Dark, System}`), `crates/ars-core/src/platform.rs` (`NullPlatformEffects`).
- Leptos component + hooks: `crates/ars-leptos/src/provider.rs` (`ArsProvider`, `ArsContext`, `use_locale`, `use_direction`, `use_style_strategy` at `attrs.rs:1226`, `use_intl_backend`, `use_modality_context`, `use_platform_effects`, `use_number_formatter`, `t`).
- Dioxus component + hooks: `crates/ars-dioxus/src/provider.rs` (same surface + `ArsProviderProps`, `arc_option_ptr_eq`, `default_dioxus_platform`).
- Nonce: `crates/ars-leptos/src/nonce.rs` and `crates/ars-dioxus/src/nonce.rs` (`ArsNonceCssCtx`, `ArsNonceStyle`, `use_nonce_css_context_provider`, `NonceCssRule`, `append_nonce_css`).
- SSR oracle pattern: `crates/ars-leptos/tests/client_only.rs` (Leptos `view!{}.to_html()`), `crates/ars-dioxus/tests/client_only.rs` (Dioxus `VirtualDom::new(app); vdom.rebuild_in_place(); dioxus_ssr::render(&vdom)`).
- WASM probe pattern: existing inline `ProviderProbe` / `ConfiguredProviderProbe` in each adapter's `provider.rs` test module — reuse the **shape** (don't import; tests/ files can't see `pub(crate)` helpers, so define a local probe).

## Files to modify

### Leptos (#347)

1. **Create** `crates/ars-leptos/tests/ars_provider.rs` — native SSR oracle, gated `#![cfg(all(not(target_arch = "wasm32"), feature = "ssr"))]`, mirroring the `client_only.rs` shape. Scenarios:
   - Renders `<div dir="ltr">` wrapper with children for default props (no locale supplied).
   - Renders `<div dir="rtl">` when an RTL locale (e.g. `Locale::parse("ar-EG")`) is provided.
   - Renders `<div dir="rtl">` when explicit `direction=Signal::stored(Direction::Rtl)` overrides an LTR locale.
   - When `style_strategy=StyleStrategy::Nonce("test-nonce")`, the SSR HTML contains a `<style nonce="test-nonce">` tag (sourced from `ArsNonceStyle`).
   - When `style_strategy=StyleStrategy::Inline` (default), no `<style nonce="…">` is emitted.

2. **Create** `crates/ars-leptos/tests/ars_provider_wasm.rs` — `#![cfg(target_arch = "wasm32")]` + `wasm_bindgen_test_configure!(run_in_browser)`. Use a locally-defined `#[component] fn Probe()` that reads `use_context::<ArsContext>()` (reuse the pattern from the existing inline `ConfiguredProviderProbe`). Scenarios that complement (don't duplicate) the inline tests:
   - All-props configuration round-trip: `locale`, `direction` override, `color_mode`, `disabled`, `read_only`, `id_prefix`, `portal_container_id`, `root_node_id`, `style_strategy=Cssom` — every field observable via the probe matches the input.
   - Nonce auto-render: when `style_strategy=StyleStrategy::Nonce(...)` is passed, a probe verifies (a) `ArsNonceCssCtx` is published and (b) the rendered DOM contains a `<style>` element with the expected nonce attribute (mount via `leptos::mount::mount_to(...)` then query).
   - `use_style_strategy()` fallback: outside any provider, returns `StyleStrategy::Inline`.

3. **Edit** `crates/ars-leptos/src/prelude.rs` — add `pub use crate::ArsProvider;` and `pub use ars_core::ColorMode;` (color mode is a configuration type users pass via the `color_mode` prop; add a short doc comment on the `ColorMode` line to explain why it's in the prelude).

### Dioxus (#477)

4. **Create** `crates/ars-dioxus/tests/ars_provider.rs` — native SSR oracle, gated `#![cfg(not(target_arch = "wasm32"))]`, using the `render_app` helper from `client_only.rs`. Scenarios:
